tutorials.title: Tutorials / Guides
tutorials.subtitle: Practical notes for keeping scenes expressive, readable, and easy to maintain.

items.title: Balancing style and memory budget
items.summary: Keep atmosphere without crowding the context window.
items.content:
Start by deciding which details must repeat and which only need to appear once. Signature tone belongs in the preset; one-off flavor should stay in the card or opening message.

- Keep reusable style cues short and portable.
- Move world trivia into lorebooks instead of the main prompt.
- Reserve long prose for openings, not every generation.

items.title: When to split a lorebook
items.summary: A simple rule for separating world notes from character notes.
items.content:
If the entry can apply to more than one character, it probably belongs in a world lorebook. If it changes how a specific character speaks, reacts, or remembers, keep it attached to the character set.

- Worldbook: places, factions, broad customs.
- Character book: habits, private history, personal vocabulary.
- Shared duos: make a mini companion file for cross-cues.

items.title: Soft formatting for exported logs
items.summary: Improve readability without making transcripts feel mechanical.
items.content:
Aim for consistency, not perfection. Use one divider style, one quote style, and one note style across the whole archive so logs feel collected rather than generated.

A light left border works well for commentary. Short hand-labeled dates, version notes, and tags also make future edits much easier.
